flashcards.front: What is the primary purpose of LinearLayout in Android?
flashcards.back: LinearLayout arranges UI components sequentially in a single row (horizontal) or column (vertical).

flashcards.front: Which attribute controls the orientation of a LinearLayout?
flashcards.back: android:orientation with values 'horizontal' or 'vertical' defines the LinearLayout's direction.

flashcards.front: Why is AbsoluteLayout deprecated in modern Android development?
flashcards.back: AbsoluteLayout uses fixed pixel coordinates, making UIs non-responsive to different screen sizes.

flashcards.front: What is the key characteristic of FrameLayout?
flashcards.back: FrameLayout stacks views on top of each other, typically used for overlays or single-element containers.

flashcards.front: How to center a view within a FrameLayout?
flashcards.back: Use android:layout_gravity='center' attribute on the child view.

flashcards.front: What problem does layout_weight solve in LinearLayout?
flashcards.back: Distributes remaining space proportionally among child views when set to 0dp width/height.

flashcards.front: Which layout is best for a login form with stacked fields?
flashcards.back: Vertical LinearLayout arranges text fields and buttons in a single column.

flashcards.front: What happens to views in FrameLayout without positioning attributes?
flashcards.back: All views stack in top-left corner, with last-added view visible on top.

flashcards.front: Name a real-world use case for FrameLayout.
flashcards.back: Displaying loading spinners over content or creating image/text overlays.

flashcards.front: How to make three buttons share equal width in horizontal LinearLayout?
flashcards.back: Set android:layout_width='0dp' and android:layout_weight='1' on each button.

flashcards.front: What is the main disadvantage of AbsoluteLayout?
flashcards.back: Requires manual pixel adjustments for different screen densities and sizes.

flashcards.front: Which layout property controls view order in FrameLayout?
flashcards.back: android:elevation (API 21+) or XML order determines stacking sequence.

flashcards.front: What's the difference between gravity and layout_gravity?
flashcards.back: gravity affects content inside a view, layout_gravity positions the view within its parent.

flashcards.front: How to create right-aligned buttons in vertical LinearLayout?
flashcards.back: Set android:layout_gravity='end' on the button views.

flashcards.front: Why is FrameLayout considered performance-efficient?
flashcards.back: It performs minimal measurement/layout calculations compared to complex layouts.

flashcards.front: What modern layout replaces AbsoluteLayout's functionality?
flashcards.back: ConstraintLayout provides flexible positioning without fixed coordinates.

flashcards.front: How to prevent overlapping in FrameLayout?
flashcards.back: Use layout_gravity and margins to position views in non-overlapping areas.

flashcards.front: Which layout is ideal for a toolbar with multiple horizontal icons?
flashcards.back: Horizontal LinearLayout with appropriate margins/padding between icons.

flashcards.front: What does android:baselineAligned do in LinearLayout?
flashcards.back: Controls whether children's baselines (text alignment) are aligned. Default is true.

flashcards.front: How to handle varying screen sizes with LinearLayout?
flashcards.back: Combine with weightSum/layout_weight and wrap_content/match_parent attributes.

flashcards.front: What is the default child view arrangement in FrameLayout?
flashcards.back: All children are positioned at the top-left corner (0,0 coordinates).

flashcards.front: When should you avoid using LinearLayout?
flashcards.back: When needing complex UIs with overlapping elements or multiple alignment constraints.

flashcards.front: What XML attribute creates space between LinearLayout items?
flashcards.back: android:divider with android:showDividers and android:dividerPadding.

flashcards.front: How to make a view take 25% of screen width in horizontal LinearLayout?
flashcards.back: Set layout_width='0dp' and layout_weight='1' with total weightSum='4' on parent.

flashcards.front: What is the primary advantage of LinearLayout over RelativeLayout?
flashcards.back: Simpler implementation for sequential element arrangements with predictable behavior.
